get.summary:
ListCloudSources returns all cloud sources matching the given filter,
sorted by name. Supports pagination via the pagination field (max 1000
results per page).

parameters.name: filter.names
parameters.description: Matches cloud sources based on their name.

parameters.name: filter.types
parameters.description:
Matches cloud sources based on their type.

 - TYPE_PALADIN_CLOUD: TYPE_PALADIN_CLOUD connects to the Paladin Cloud API for cluster discovery.
 - TYPE_OCM: TYPE_OCM connects to the Red Hat OpenShift Cluster Manager for cluster discovery.

post.summary: CreateCloudSource creates a new cloud source.
post.description:
The cloud_source.id field must be empty. Unless skip_test_integration is
true, the cloud source is tested for connectivity before being persisted.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is set, if the cloud source is empty, or
if the connectivity test fails.

post.summary:
TestCloudSource verifies connectivity for the given cloud source
configuration using a short timeout (8 seconds) and no retries.
post.description:
When update_credentials is false, the stored credentials for the existing
cloud source are used. When update_credentials is true, the credentials in
the request are used directly.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the cloud source is empty, if credential
reconciliation fails, or if the connectivity test fails.

put.summary: UpdateCloudSource replaces an existing cloud source configuration.
put.description:
When update_credentials is false, credentials in the request are ignored
and the stored credentials are used instead (the resource must already
exist). Unless skip_test_integration is true, connectivity is verified
before the update is persisted.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the cloud source is empty, if credential
reconciliation fails, or if the connectivity test fails.

parameters.name: cloudSource.id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the cloud source, assigned on creation.

get.summary: GetCloudSource retrieves a single cloud source by ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no cloud source with the given ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the cloud source to retrieve.

delete.summary: DeleteCloudSource removes the cloud source with the given ID.
delete.description: Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the cloud source to delete.

get.summary:
CountCloudSources returns the number of cloud sources matching the given
filter. Supports filtering by name or type.

parameters.name: filter.names
parameters.description: Matches cloud sources based on their name.

parameters.name: filter.types
parameters.description:
Matches cloud sources based on their type.

 - TYPE_PALADIN_CLOUD: TYPE_PALADIN_CLOUD connects to the Paladin Cloud API for cluster discovery.
 - TYPE_OCM: TYPE_OCM connects to the Red Hat OpenShift Cluster Manager for cluster discovery.

secret.description: Used for single-valued authentication via long-lived tokens.

clientId.description: Used for client authentication in combination with client_secret.

clientSecret.description: Used for client authentication in combination with client_id.

name.description: name is a human-readable label for the cloud source.

credentials.description: credentials holds the authentication material for the cloud source endpoint.

skipTestIntegration.description:
skip_test_integration, when true, skips connectivity verification when creating
or updating this cloud source.

cloudSource.description: cloud_source contains the updated configuration. The id field must be set.
cloudSource.title: cloud_source contains the updated configuration. The id field must be set.

updateCredentials.description:
If true, cloud_source must include valid credentials.
If false, the resource must already exist and
credentials in cloud_source are ignored.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

id.description: id is the unique identifier of the cloud source, assigned on creation.

name.description: name is a human-readable label for the cloud source.

credentials.description: credentials holds the authentication material for the cloud source endpoint.

skipTestIntegration.description:
skip_test_integration, when true, skips connectivity verification when creating
or updating this cloud source.

v1CloudSource.description:
CloudSource is an integration which provides a source for discovered
clusters.

v1CloudSourceType.description:
Type identifies which cloud provider or manager the source connects to.

 - TYPE_PALADIN_CLOUD: TYPE_PALADIN_CLOUD connects to the Paladin Cloud API for cluster discovery.
 - TYPE_OCM: TYPE_OCM connects to the Red Hat OpenShift Cluster Manager for cluster discovery.

names.description: Matches cloud sources based on their name.

types.description: Matches cloud sources based on their type.

count.description: count is the total number of cloud sources matching the request filter.

cloudSource.description:
cloud_source contains the configuration for the new cloud source.
The id field must be empty.
cloud_source is the created cloud source with its assigned id.
cloud_source is the requested cloud source configuration.

cloudSources.description:
cloud_sources contains the matching cloud source configurations, sorted by
name in ascending order.

endpoint.title:
Endpoint to the OpenShift API server. Https is assumed if no
protocol is specified. Example: https://api.openshift.com

v1OCMConfig.description:
OCMConfig provides information required to fetch discovered clusters from
the OpenShift cluster manager.

sortOptions.description: This field is under development. It is not supported on any REST APIs.

endpoint.title:
Endpoint to the Paladin Cloud API server. Https is assumed if no
protocol is specified. Example: https://apiqa.paladincloud.io

v1PaladinCloudConfig.description:
PaladinCloudConfig provides information required to fetch discovered
clusters from Paladin Cloud.

aggregateBy.description: This field is under development. It is not supported on any REST APIs.

cloudSource.description: cloud_source contains the configuration to test.

updateCredentials.description:
If true, cloud_source must include valid credentials.
If false, the resource must already exist and
credentials in cloud_source are ignored.
